mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20d returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and residential investment, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show positive 60d returns in more than 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative 20d returns in more than 60% of observations during early-cycle periods, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index growth erodes real corporate margins and triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months show positive 5d returns in more than 50% of cases with Rank IC exceeding 0.04, invalidating the margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Gross domestic product growth falling below 1.0 percent quarter-over-quarter indicates severe economic stagnation that reduces corporate profit forecasts and causes negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.0% is followed by positive 60d returns in more than 60% of instances, or the signal fails to predict direction in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion and rising household income, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent payroll gains and unemployment drops yield negative 20d returns in more than 55% of cases, or the joint signal shows zero predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates below 1.0 percent create an ultra-loose financial environment that forces investors into equities for yield, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Sub-1% rate environments produce negative 60d returns in more than 60% of cases, or the yield-seeking channel shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample periods.
